summary.intent: Fix selection normalization bugs in Select and Listbox components (PR #665 on fogodev/ars-ui) by addressing Codex review threads about stale key guards, disabled state blocking, and hidden input naming.
summary.outcome: Two rounds of Codex review threads were addressed: first fixing selection normalization on prop sync and initialization (commit cedfb6ee), then fixing stale-event guards on select/deselect paths, listbox disabled-state activation blocking, and Select hidden input name attribute (second commit). All six threads from each round were resolved, CI passed all gates, and the final push was made.

repo: The repo uses Codex bot for automated PR review; triggering re-review requires posting '@codex review' as a PR comment after pushing fixes.
CI pipeline has multiple sequential waves: formatting/snapshot/workspace/clippy → unit/integration/DOM/i18n/feature-core → adapter/parity → coverage/release-verification/feature-flag matrix.
Review threads are resolved via GitHub GraphQL `resolveReviewThread` mutation, not through the REST API.
Selection normalization must happen both at initialization (controlled + default keys) and on prop sync (SyncProps after applying next mode).
Controlled selection pruning must write through the current controlled mirror, not just the internal bindable slot.
PropTest suite for state machines is run with PROPTEST_CASES=256 and requires --ignored flag.

code.finding: Listbox select/deselect paths needed stale key guards to avoid acting on keys no longer present in the collection.
Listbox item activation was not blocking when the component is disabled; needed an early return guard.
Listbox controlled selection pruning needed to keep controlled binding reads and selection_state.selected_keys in sync.
Select select/deselect paths needed stale key guards matching the listbox fix.
Select hidden input was rendering with name="" (empty string) instead of the configured name prop.
Select controlled selection pruning writes must go through the current controlled mirror, not only the internal bindable slot.

workflow: Polling CI via `gh pr checks` with `sleep` intervals is necessary since there is no push-based notification for remote CI completion in this workflow.
Replying to and resolving review threads in a single batched shell script (reply + resolve per thread) is efficient for multi-thread Codex rounds.
Running `cargo +nightly fmt --all`, focused unit tests, proptest state machines, `cargo check` with feature flags, and clippy before each commit prevents CI surprises.
Snapshot tests did not regenerate because the hidden input fix used an already-named field — snapshot paths are sensitive to field naming conventions.

friction: CI polling required multiple manual sleep-and-recheck loops (30s, 60s, 90s, 120s, 180s intervals) with no automated notification mechanism.
Codex posted a second round of six review threads only after the full CI run completed, requiring another full fix-test-push cycle.
Locating the exact line ranges for patches required multiple `sed -n` and `nl -ba` invocations to orient within large files before applying patches.
The repo pre-commit hook ran silently with no output, causing uncertainty about whether it had completed before the push.
open_items: Coverage, release verification, and feature-flag matrix CI jobs were still pending at the time of the second push — their final status was not confirmed in the transcript.
Codex had only '👀' (watching) status after the second push; a final thumbs-up approval from Codex was not yet received.
No confirmation that the PR was merged or approved by a human reviewer.
